name: Validation

unions.name: Validation
unions.comment:
It can fail with one or more errors. The major difference between `Validation` and `Result` is that
when validations are composed their errors accumulate.

values.name: allErrors
values.type: Validation.Validation e a -> List.List e

values.name: andThen
values.comment:
Chain together a sequence of validations that may fail.

We only continue with the callback if things are going well.


    toInt : String -> Validation String Int
    toInt rawString =
        fromMaybe "cannot convert to an Int" (String.toInt rawString)

    toMonthFromInt : Int -> Validation String Int
    toMonthFromInt anyInt =
        if anyInt >= 1 && anyInt <= 12 then
            succeed anyInt

        else
            fail "months must be between 1 and 12"

    toMonthFromString : String -> Validation String Int
    toMonthFromString rawString =
        toInt rawString
            |> andThen toMonthFromInt

    -- toMonthFromString "4" == succeed 4
    -- toMonthFromString "9" == succeed 9
    -- toMonthFromString "a" == fail "cannot convert to an Int"
    -- toMonthFromString "0" == fail "months must be between 1 and 12"
values.type: (a -> Validation.Validation x b) -> Validation.Validation x a -> Validation.Validation x b

values.name: apply
values.comment:
Adds support for an applicative style of programming.

Practically, it means that when you need to go beyond `map5` you can use this function together with [`succeed`](#succeed) to achieve it. For e.g.

    succeed (\a b c d e f g h i j k -> ...)
        |> apply va
        |> apply vb
        |> apply vc
        |> apply vd
        |> apply ve
        |> apply vf
        |> apply vg
        |> apply vh
        |> apply vi
        |> apply vj
        |> apply vk
values.type: Validation.Validation x a -> Validation.Validation x (a -> b) -> Validation.Validation x b

values.name: fail
values.type: e -> Validation.Validation e a

values.name: failWithErrors
values.type: e -> List.List e -> Validation.Validation e a

values.name: firstError
values.comment:
firstError (succeed 5) == Nothing

    firstError (fail "x") == Just "x"

    firstError (failWithErrors "x" [ "y", "z" ]) == Just "x"
values.type: Validation.Validation e a -> Maybe.Maybe e

values.name: fromMaybe
values.type: e -> Maybe.Maybe a -> Validation.Validation e a

values.name: fromResult
values.type: Result.Result e a -> Validation.Validation e a

values.name: isInvalid
values.type: Validation.Validation e a -> Basics.Bool

values.name: isValid
values.type: Validation.Validation e a -> Basics.Bool

values.name: lastError
values.comment:
lastError (succeed 5) == Nothing

    lastError (fail "x") == Just "x"

    lastError (failWithErrors "x" [ "y", "z" ]) == Just "z"
values.type: Validation.Validation e a -> Maybe.Maybe e

values.name: map
values.comment:
Apply a function to a validation. If the validation is a success, the value will be converted. If the validation is
a failure, the same error will be propagated.

    map sqrt (succeed 4.0) == succeed 2.0

    map sqrt (fail "bad input") == fail "bad input"
values.type: (a -> value) -> Validation.Validation x a -> Validation.Validation x value

values.name: map2
values.comment:
Apply a function if both validations are a success. If not, the errors will be accumulated in order of failure.

    map2 max (succeed 42) (succeed 13) == succeed 42

    map2 max (fail "x") (succeed 13) == fail "x"

    map2 max (succeed 42) (fail "y") == fail "y"

    map2 max (fail "x") (fail "y") == failWithErrors "x" [ "y" ]
values.type: (a -> b -> value) -> Validation.Validation x a -> Validation.Validation x b -> Validation.Validation x value

values.name: map3
values.comment:
Apply a function if all three validations are a success. If not, the errors will be accumulated in order of failure.

    map3 (\a b c -> a + b + c) (succeed 1) (succeed 2) (succeed 3) == succeed 6

    map3 (\a b c -> a + b + c) (succeed 1) (fail "y") (succeed 3) == fail "y"

    map3 (\a b c -> a + b + c) (fail "x") (succeed 2) (fail "z") == failWithErrors "x" [ "z" ]

    map3 (\a b c -> a + b + c) (fail "x") (fail "y") (fail "z") == failWithErrors "x" [ "y", "z" ]
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> value) -> Validation.Validation x a -> Validation.Validation x b -> Validation.Validation x c -> Validation.Validation x value

values.name: map4
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> value) -> Validation.Validation x a -> Validation.Validation x b -> Validation.Validation x c -> Validation.Validation x d -> Validation.Validation x value

values.name: map5
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> e -> value) -> Validation.Validation x a -> Validation.Validation x b -> Validation.Validation x c -> Validation.Validation x d -> Validation.Validation x e -> Validation.Validation x value

values.name: mapError
values.type: (x -> y) -> Validation.Validation x a -> Validation.Validation y a

values.name: succeed
values.type: a -> Validation.Validation e a

values.name: toMaybe
values.comment:
toMaybe (succeed 5) == Just 5

    toMaybe (fail "x") == Nothing

    toMaybe (failWithErrors "x" [ "y" ]) == Nothing
values.type: Validation.Validation e a -> Maybe.Maybe a

values.name: toResult
values.comment:
toResult (succeed 5) == Ok 5

    toResult (fail "x") == Err [ "x" ]

    toResult (failWithErrors "x" [ "y" ]) == Err [ "x", "y" ]
values.type: Validation.Validation e a -> Result.Result (List.List e) a

values.name: withDefault
values.type: a -> Validation.Validation e a -> a
